seo.title: API documentation | Taimbox
seo.description: Taimbox integration API documentation. Connect your tools to planning, team, and project data.

toastLinkCopiedDesc: Paste it to share this section

intro.title: Integration API
intro.subtitle: Connect your agency’s planning, team, and project data in Taimbox with your internal tools.
intro.tokenBody: Each agency’s admins can create access tokens from API & integrations in the app. Each token is tied to your agency_id and protected by RLS (Row Level Security) policies.
intro.tokenRls: All operations are scoped to your agency’s data. You cannot access other agencies’ data.
useCases: Sync projects and employees with your ERP or CRM
Query planned vs. actual hours by week or month
Create employees, clients, projects, and absences from integrations
Build custom reports from planning data
Read assignments and pending transfers (task writes require the app)
Receive live updates via Realtime subscriptions

auth.title: Authentication & security
auth.intro: Every request needs two header values:
auth.apikeyBody: The anonymous key (ANON_KEY) for your Supabase instance—the same one the web app uses. Send it in the apikey header.
auth.authBody: JWT token created in <0>API & integrations. It contains your agency’s agency_id. Send it as Bearer <token>.
steps: Sign in to Taimbox as an admin of your agency.
Open Settings → API & integrations in the sidebar.
Click Create token, pick a clear name, and choose read-only or read/write permissions. Read-only tokens return 403 on POST, PATCH, and DELETE.
Copy the JWT shown. It is shown only once—store it somewhere safe.

security.title: Do not expose tokens
security.text: Never put tokens in public repos, logs, or client-side code.

security.title: Rotate regularly
security.text: Revoke and reissue tokens periodically. Revoke immediately if you suspect compromise.

security.title: Least privilege
security.text: Create tokens with only the permissions you need. Use read-only when you do not write.

security.title: Use expiry
security.text: When creating a token you can set expiry days. Non-expiring tokens are riskier.

auth.rlsBody: RLS policies scope each request to your token’s tenant (agency_id in the JWT). Some tables have no direct agency_id or restrict access to the employee linked to the signed-in user. Read RLS limits & permissions before integrating.
auth.rateBody: There are no hard limits yet, but stay under ~100 requests/minute. Formal limits may be added later.

baseUrl.title: Base URL & headers
baseUrl.intro: All requests go to the Taimbox API URL. You need your Supabase ANON_KEY (same as the app) and an API_TOKEN from API & integrations.
baseUrl.codeBlock:
# Taimbox API base URL
https://api.taimbox.com/rest/v1/

# Required headers on every request
apikey: <ANON_KEY>                      # Anonymous key for your Supabase project
Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_TOKEN>  # Token from API & integrations
Content-Type: application/json
Prefer: return=representation           # Return created/updated rows in the body
baseUrl.importantBody: Your token is tied to your agency via a signed JWT. You can only read and write your own agency’s data thanks to RLS. Revoke tokens from API & integrations.
baseUrl.agencyIdTitle: When do I need the agency ID?
baseUrl.agencyIdRead: On reads (GET): Never. The token already identifies your agency and RLS filters automatically. You do not need agency_id in filters.
baseUrl.agencyIdWrite: On inserts (POST): When creating employees, clients, projects, or other rows with agency_id, include that field in the body. Copy it from API & integrations (Connection data → Agency ID). It matches the token JWT’s agency_id claim. Do not use GET /agencies (returns []); RPC list_my_agencies_directory is optional and returns id + name only—mainly for multi-agency app sessions.

rls.title: RLS limits & permissions
rls.intro: Not every table behaves the same with an API token. This table summarizes what you can read and write from server-to-server integrations vs. what requires an app user session.
rls.tokenNote: API tokens identify the agency but not a specific employee. Timer, transfer acceptance, and assignment edits are therefore unavailable with integration Bearer tokens.
rls.permissionsBody: When creating a token you choose read-only (readonly) or read/write (readwrite). Readonly tokens get 403 on POST, PATCH, and DELETE. Readwrite tokens can write only where RLS allows: employees, clients, projects, absences, deadlines, task_transfers (create request), etc. They do not unlock allocations or app RPCs.

rows.filter: agency_id from token UI; do not query agencies

outOfScope.name: notification_rules / notification_deliveries
outOfScope.reason: Internal notification config; deliveries are service role only.

outOfScope.name: ad_accounts_config, google_ads_*, meta_ads_*, segmentation_rules
outOfScope.reason: Ads integrations managed in the app, not in the public API.

outOfScope.name: platform_admins, admin_*, support_tickets, review_*
outOfScope.reason: Platform admin, support, and Review Agents—outside agency integration.

outOfScope.name: api_tokens
outOfScope.reason: Create/revoke tokens only from the app (Edge Functions generate-api-token / revoke-api-token).

responses.title: Responses & errors
responses.intro: All responses are JSON. The API uses standard HTTP codes and structured errors. Always check the SDK’s error field or the HTTP status on raw requests.
responses.empty200Body: A valid GET (good token) may return [] with 200 when no rows match or RLS hides every row. You only get 401/403 for invalid tokens or missing permission. Treat 200 + empty array as “no results”, not an error.

formatCards.title: GET list
formatCards.desc: JSON array of objects. It may be empty when there are no matches or RLS hides all rows (still 200—by design).

formatCards.title: GET/POST/PATCH one
formatCards.desc: Single JSON object when using Prefer: return=representation.

formatCards.title: DELETE
formatCards.desc: No response body. Confirm status is 204.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.intro: API change history. Check here for new features, improvements, and possible breaking changes.
changelog.empty: No changelog entries yet. Changes will be documented here.

entries.title: Major API documentation update
entries.description: Tutorial chain expanded to 11 guides with 1:1 reference coverage (clients/projects, task notes, time tracking, transfers, global assignments, configuration, feedback, goals, and editing locks). Reference and RLS aligned: agencies as non-integratable tenant schema, per-table limits, readonly/readwrite permissions, and RPCs marked app-only where applicable.

entries.title: allocation_notes and weekly_action
entries.description: Reference for allocation_notes (per-task notes) and weekly_action on weekly_feedback. New transfer columns on allocations (distribution_source_allocation_id, original_transferred_task_name).

entries.title: accept_task_transfer RPC
entries.description: Atomic transfer acceptance (keep, move, distribute, rollover modes). Documented under REST; callable only by the authenticated receiver in the app.

entries.title: Time tables with agency_id and documented RPCs
entries.description: Adds agency_id to time_entries, active_timers, and timer_sessions so you can filter by agency in the API. Documents active_timers and timer_sessions in the resource reference, and the log_timer_hours (log hours when stopping the timer) and get_team_active_timers (list team active timers) RPCs in the REST section.

entries.title: API documentation v1
entries.description: First public release of the integration API docs: resource reference, tutorials, JavaScript SDK, Realtime subscriptions, sample responses, and readonly/readwrite permissions.

entries.title: Per-agency API tokens
entries.description: Admins can create and revoke JWT tokens in API & integrations. Each token is tied to an agency_id, supports read-only or read/write scopes, and is protected by RLS policies.

filtering.title: Filtering, pagination & sorting
filtering.orderCode:
// SDK
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .select('*')
  .order('name', { ascending: true })
  .order('created_at', { ascending: false })

// HTTP: order=name.asc,created_at.desc
filtering.fullIntro: Fetch February 2026 allocations for an employee, sorted by week:
filtering.fullCode:
const { data, error, count } = await taimbox
  .from('allocations')
  .select('id, project_id, week_start_date, hours_assigned, task_name, status', { count: 'exact' })
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .gte('week_start_date', '2026-02-01')
  .lte('week_start_date', '2026-02-28')
  .eq('status', 'planned')
  .order('week_start_date')
  .range(0, 49)

// count = total matching rows (for pagination)
// data = allocation rows

realtime.title: Realtime subscriptions
realtime.intro: Stream changes without polling—great for dashboards that refresh when allocations or absences change.
realtime.noteBody: Realtime is enabled on planning and team tables: allocations, employees, projects, clients, task_transfers, absences, time_entries (own rows), allocation_notes, and others per supabase_realtime publication. API tokens can subscribe with the same JWT. Contact us if you need a specific table.

sdk.title: JavaScript SDK (optional)
sdk.intro: Use plain HTTP (see REST) or, with JS/TS, the Supabase client for a nicer API.
sdk.noteBody: On tables with agency_id you do not need to filter on GET: RLS uses the JWT agency_id. For allocations, deadlines, or absences filter by employee_id or project_id.

rest.title: REST API (HTTP)
rest.intro: Call the API from any language. Every resource follows the same REST pattern.
rest.getDesc: List resources with optional filters. Supports select, filters, pagination, and ordering.
rest.getSdk:
const { data, error } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .select('id, name, role')
  .eq('is_active', true)
  .order('name')
rest.postDesc: Create a resource. Send JSON in the body.
rest.postSdk:
const { data, error } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .insert({
    name: 'Ana Garcia',
    role: 'Designer',
    default_weekly_capacity: 40,
    agency_id: '<AGENCY_ID>',
    work_schedule: { monday: 8, tuesday: 8, wednesday: 8, thursday: 8, friday: 8, saturday: 0, sunday: 0 }
  })
  .select()
  .single()
rest.patchDesc: Update one or more fields on an existing resource.
rest.patchSdk:
const { data, error } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .update({ default_weekly_capacity: 32 })
  .eq('id', employeeId)
  .select()
  .single()
rest.deleteDesc: Delete a resource. This cannot be undone.
rest.rpcIntegrationsIntro: Functions callable with your integration Bearer within RLS limits.
rest.listAgenciesDesc: Optional. Returns id and name only (no settings) for agencies scoped to your token. For server-to-server integrations the ID from API & Integrations is usually enough; this RPC helps more with app sessions and multiple agencies.
rest.listAgenciesSdk:
const { data } = await taimbox.rpc('list_my_agencies_directory')
// [{ id: "...", name: "My Agency" }]
rest.rpcAppIntro: Require signing in to Taimbox with a linked employee. They do not work with integration API tokens.
rest.logTimerDesc: Stops the timer and logs hours: UPSERT into time_entries, updates allocations.hours_actual, inserts timer_sessions, clears active_timers. Only the employee linked to auth.uid(). Params: p_employee_id, p_allocation_id, p_hours, p_notes (optional), p_date (optional).
rest.teamTimersDesc: Lists active timers for the authenticated user’s agency. Returns employee_id, employee_name, allocation_id, task_name, client_name, started_at. App session only; no parameters.
rest.acceptTransferDesc: Atomically accepts a pending transfer. Receiver only (app session). Params: p_transfer_id, p_acceptance_mode (keep|move|distribute|rollover), p_result_allocation_ids (optional), p_target_week (required for move). Returns JSON result.

quickStart.title: Getting started
quickStart.subtitle: Within five minutes you’ll have your first request working: get a token, configure the client, and verify connectivity.
quickStart.prereq2: Node.js 18+ (for the SDK) or any HTTP client (cURL, Postman, …)
quickStart.nextText: Now that the connection works, learn how to

steps.title: Generate your API token
steps.description: Open Settings > API & integrations in Taimbox and create a token. Give it a clear name like “My integration” and pick read permissions.
steps.note: The token is shown only once—copy it to a secure place (e.g. an environment variable).

steps.title: Install the SDK (optional)
steps.description: If you use JavaScript/TypeScript, install the Supabase client to simplify queries.
steps.code: npm install @supabase/supabase-js

steps.title: Configure the client
steps.description: Keep credentials in config or env vars—never hard-code tokens in source.
steps.note: If you only use HTTP (cURL, Postman, Python requests, …), skip this step: use the base URL plus apikey and Authorization on every call. Jump to step 5 for a cURL sample.

steps.title: Make your first request
steps.description: List active employees to confirm everything works.
steps.note: 401 means bad ANON_KEY or API token. An empty array may simply mean no active employees yet.

steps.title: Plain cURL works too
steps.description: Without JavaScript you can hit the API directly.
steps.code:
curl -s \
  'https://api.taimbox.com/rest/v1/employees?is_active=eq.true&limit=5&select=id,name,role' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN' | json_pp

syncTeam.title: Sync your team
syncTeam.subtitle: Read and write employees, clients, and projects from your ERP, CRM, or other external tool.
syncTeam.nextText: With clients and projects synced, learn to

steps.title: Read every employee
steps.description: Fetch all active employees with their core fields.
steps.note: RLS scopes results to your agency—no agency_id filter needed on reads.
steps.code:
const { data: employees, error } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .select('id, name, email, role, default_weekly_capacity, work_schedule, is_active')
  .eq('is_active', true)
  .order('name')

// => array of employees
// [{ id: "e1-...", name: "Jane Doe", email: "jane@...", role: "Designer", ... }]

steps.title: Create a new employee
steps.description: Required fields: name, role, default_weekly_capacity, work_schedule, and agency_id (copy from API & integrations → Connection data).

steps.title: Update working hours
steps.description: Adjust weekly capacity or per-day hours when schedules change.

steps.title: Deactivate an employee
steps.description: Prefer deactivating over deleting to keep historical allocations.
steps.note: Inactive employees keep past allocations. Avoid DELETE unless you truly need to purge records.
steps.code:
const { error } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .update({ is_active: false })
  .eq('id', employeeId)

// Hidden from planning and active views

steps.title: List and create clients
steps.description: Sync your commercial portfolio. Each client needs name, color (hex), and agency_id.
steps.code:
const { data: clients } = await taimbox
  .from('clients')
  .select('id, name, color')
  .order('name')

const { data: newClient } = await taimbox
  .from('clients')
  .insert({ name: 'Acme Corp', color: '#3B82F6', agency_id: '<AGENCY_ID>' })
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Create linked projects
steps.description: Projects belong to a client_id. You can set budget, fee, and deliverable dates.
steps.note: Use external_id to map your CRM record.
steps.code:
const { data: project } = await taimbox
  .from('projects')
  .insert({
    client_id: clientId,
    name: 'Q2 2026 Campaign',
    budget_hours: 80,
    monthly_fee: 2400,
    project_type: 'Monthly',
    agency_id: '<AGENCY_ID>',
    external_id: 1042
  })
  .select()
  .single()

planning.title: Automate planning
planning.subtitle: Query weekly allocations, deadlines, and actual hours. Writing tasks (allocations) requires the app; with an API token you can read and export.

steps.title: Read existing allocations
steps.description: Fetch planned work for an employee between two dates.
steps.code:
const { data: allocations } = await taimbox
  .from('allocations')
  .select(`
    id, employee_id, project_id, week_start_date,
    hours_assigned, hours_actual, task_name, status
  `)
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .gte('week_start_date', '2026-03-01')
  .lte('week_start_date', '2026-03-31')
  .order('week_start_date')

// Result: allocations for March

steps.title: Create an allocation
steps.description: Assign an employee’s hours to a project for a specific week. week_start_date should be a Monday.
steps.note: Writing allocations requires an app user session (can_assign_tasks_for_employee). Integration API tokens cannot create or edit tasks—use the app or read-only integration.
steps.code:
// Read-only with API token:
const { data: allocation } = await taimbox
  .from('allocations')
  .select('id, employee_id, project_id, week_start_date, hours_assigned, task_name')
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .eq('week_start_date', '2026-03-02')
  .maybeSingle()

steps.title: Spread hours across weeks
steps.description: Query several weeks of a project to compare planned load.
steps.note: Creating rows in bulk requires the Taimbox app (planner).
steps.code:
const weeks = ['2026-03-02', '2026-03-09', '2026-03-16', '2026-03-23']

const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('allocations')
  .select('week_start_date, hours_assigned, hours_actual')
  .eq('project_id', projectId)
  .in('week_start_date', weeks)
  .order('week_start_date')

console.log('Hours per week:', data)

steps.title: Update actual hours
steps.description: Query hours_actual on completed allocations.
steps.note: PATCH on allocations returns 403 with API tokens. Timer logging uses log_timer_hours RPC from the app.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('allocations')
  .select('id, hours_assigned, hours_actual, status')
  .eq('project_id', projectId)
  .eq('status', 'completed')
  .gte('week_start_date', '2026-03-01')

steps.title: Monthly targets (deadlines)
steps.description: Set how many hours each employee owes a project in a month.
steps.note: budget_override replaces the project’s default budget for that month only. Null falls back to project.budget_hours.

steps.title: Task notes (allocation_notes)
steps.description: Append-only comments on an allocation. Different from weekly_feedback.comments and time_entries.notes.
steps.note: Soft delete uses deleted_at in the app; filter with .is('deleted_at', null) in API queries.
steps.code:
const { data: notes } = await taimbox
  .from('allocation_notes')
  .select('id, body, author_employee_id, created_at')
  .eq('allocation_id', allocationId)
  .is('deleted_at', null)
  .order('created_at')

const { data: note } = await taimbox
  .from('allocation_notes')
  .insert({
    allocation_id: allocationId,
    agency_id: '<AGENCY_ID>',
    author_employee_id: employeeId,
    body: 'Upload ES images and EN/DE copy',
    source: 'user'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

transfers.title: Task transfers
transfers.subtitle: Create and track transfer requests between employees. You can automate creation and monitoring via API; atomic acceptance (keep, move, distribute, rollover) happens in the app.

steps.title: List pending transfers
steps.description: Fetch open requests for your agency to sync with an external system or custom dashboard.
steps.note: Filter by status pending. RLS scopes to your token’s agency_id.
steps.code:
const { data: pending } = await taimbox
  .from('task_transfers')
  .select('id, allocation_id, from_employee_id, to_employee_id, hours_transferred, status, reason, requested_at')
  .eq('status', 'pending')
  .order('requested_at', { ascending: false })

console.log(`${pending?.length ?? 0} pending transfers`)

steps.title: Create a request
steps.description: An employee asks to move task hours to a teammate. The receiver must accept in Taimbox (accept_task_transfer RPC).
steps.note: Requires a readwrite token. Only one pending request per allocation_id.
steps.code:
const { data: transfer, error } = await taimbox
  .from('task_transfers')
  .insert({
    allocation_id: allocationId,
    from_employee_id: fromEmployeeId,
    to_employee_id: toEmployeeId,
    hours_transferred: 4,
    reason: 'Overloaded this week',
    agency_id: '<AGENCY_ID>'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

if (!error) console.log('Request created:', transfer.id)

steps.title: Cancel a request
steps.description: The sender can cancel a transfer that is still pending before the receiver responds.
steps.code:
const { error } = await taimbox
  .from('task_transfers')
  .update({ status: 'cancelled' })
  .eq('id', transferId)
  .eq('status', 'pending')

if (!error) console.log('Transfer cancelled')

steps.title: Query history by status
steps.description: Export accepted, rejected, or cancelled transfers for audit or reporting.
steps.note: After acceptance, check result_allocation_ids on the row for child or continuation allocations.
steps.code:
const { data: accepted } = await taimbox
  .from('task_transfers')
  .select('id, status, acceptance_mode, result_allocation_ids, responded_at')
  .eq('status', 'accepted')
  .gte('responded_at', '2026-03-01')
  .order('responded_at', { ascending: false })

steps.title: Acceptance in the app
steps.description: Modes keep, move, distribute, and rollover change allocations atomically. Only the authenticated receiver can do this.
steps.note: Documented under REST → App RPCs (accept_task_transfer). Server-to-server integrations cannot replace Taimbox acceptance.

reports.title: Export reports
reports.subtitle: Query planning data for hour, profitability, and team utilization reports.

steps.title: Hours per project per month
steps.description: Pull every allocation in a month and aggregate by project.

steps.title: Profitability snapshot
steps.description: Join hours with project budget and monthly fee.
steps.code:
const { data: projects } = await taimbox
  .from('projects')
  .select('id, name, budget_hours, monthly_fee')
  .eq('status', 'active')

const report = projects?.map(p => {
  const hours = byProject[p.id] || { planned: 0, actual: 0, computed: 0 }
  const progress = p.budget_hours > 0
    ? Math.round((hours.computed / p.budget_hours) * 100)
    : 0
  const hourValue = p.budget_hours > 0
    ? (p.monthly_fee / p.budget_hours).toFixed(2)
    : '0'

  return {
    project: p.name,
    budgetHours: p.budget_hours,
    hoursPlanned: hours.planned,
    hoursActual: hours.actual,
    progress: `${progress}%`,
    hourValue: `${hourValue} EUR/h`
  }
})

console.table(report)

steps.title: Team utilization
steps.description: Measure each employee’s load to spot overload or idle time.
steps.code:
const { data: employees } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .select('id, name, default_weekly_capacity')
  .eq('is_active', true)

const { data: monthAllocations } = await taimbox
  .from('allocations')
  .select('employee_id, hours_assigned')
  .gte('week_start_date', '2026-02-01')
  .lte('week_start_date', '2026-02-28')

const occupancy = employees?.map(emp => {
  const totalHours = monthAllocations
    ?.filter(a => a.employee_id === emp.id)
    .reduce((sum, a) => sum + a.hours_assigned, 0) || 0
  const capacity = emp.default_weekly_capacity * 4
  const load = capacity > 0 ? Math.round((totalHours / capacity) * 100) : 0

  return { name: emp.name, totalHours, capacity, load: `${load}%` }
})

console.table(occupancy)

steps.title: Export to CSV
steps.description: Serialize rows for Excel or Sheets.

absences.title: Manage absences
absences.subtitle: Record PTO, sick days, team events, and global capacity assignments.

steps.title: Read an employee’s absences
steps.description: List absences that overlap a date range.
steps.code:
const { data: absences } = await taimbox
  .from('absences')
  .select('id, employee_id, start_date, end_date, type, hours, description')
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .gte('end_date', '2026-03-01')
  .order('start_date')

// March PTO / leave for this employee

steps.title: Create a vacation block
steps.description: Insert a multi-day vacation; capacity drops automatically in planning.
steps.note: Common type strings: vacation, sick, leave, training—but any text works.
steps.code:
const { data: absence, error } = await taimbox
  .from('absences')
  .insert({
    employee_id: employeeId,
    start_date: '2026-04-06',
    end_date: '2026-04-10',
    type: 'vacation',
    description: 'Spring break'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

if (!error) {
  console.log('Absence saved:', absence.id)
}

steps.title: Partial-day absence
steps.description: Use the hours field when the whole day is not off.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('absences')
  .insert({
    employee_id: employeeId,
    start_date: '2026-03-15',
    end_date: '2026-03-15',
    type: 'leave',
    hours: 4,
    description: 'Morning doctor visit'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

// Only 4h removed that day

steps.title: Team-wide event (holiday)
steps.description: team_events can target many employees at once.
steps.code:
const { data: employees } = await taimbox
  .from('employees')
  .select('id')
  .eq('is_active', true)

const employeeIds = employees?.map(e => e.id) || []

const { data: event } = await taimbox
  .from('team_events')
  .insert({
    name: 'Regional holiday',
    date: '2026-02-28',
    hours_reduction: 8,
    affected_employee_ids: employeeIds,
    description: 'Office closed'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

console.log('Event created for', employeeIds.length, 'people')

steps.title: Estimate real capacity
steps.description: Combine absences, events, and base weekly hours for a rough net capacity.
steps.note: Taimbox uses a finer daily model internally—this is a simplified illustration.
steps.code:
const weekStart = '2026-03-09'
const weekEnd = '2026-03-13'

const [{ data: absences }, { data: events }, { data: employee }] = await Promise.all([
  taimbox.from('absences')
    .select('start_date, end_date, hours')
    .eq('employee_id', empId)
    .lte('start_date', weekEnd)
    .gte('end_date', weekStart),
  taimbox.from('team_events')
    .select('date, hours_reduction')
    .gte('date', weekStart)
    .lte('date', weekEnd),
  taimbox.from('employees')
    .select('default_weekly_capacity')
    .eq('id', empId)
    .single()
])

const baseCapacity = employee?.default_weekly_capacity || 40
const absenceHours = absences?.reduce((s, a) => s + (a.hours || 8), 0) || 0
const eventHours = events?.reduce((s, e) => s + e.hours_reduction, 0) || 0
const effectiveCapacity = Math.max(0, baseCapacity - Math.max(absenceHours, eventHours))

console.log(`Effective capacity: ${effectiveCapacity}h / ${baseCapacity}h`)

steps.title: Global capacity assignments
steps.description: Reserve monthly hours for training, fixed meetings, or other cross-team load (global_assignments).
steps.note: With affects_all: true it applies to everyone; otherwise use affected_employee_ids.
steps.code:
const { data: globals } = await taimbox
  .from('global_assignments')
  .select('id, month, name, hours, affects_all')
  .eq('agency_id', '<AGENCY_ID>')
  .eq('month', '2026-03')

const { data: training } = await taimbox
  .from('global_assignments')
  .insert({
    month: '2026-03',
    name: 'Monthly training',
    hours: 4,
    affects_all: true,
    agency_id: '<AGENCY_ID>'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

timeTracking.title: Time tracking
timeTracking.subtitle: Query time_entries and understand timer limits (active_timers, timer_sessions, log_timer_hours RPC).

steps.title: Read time entries
steps.description: time_entries stores actual hours per allocation and day (UNIQUE employee + allocation + date).
steps.note: With an API token you only see rows RLS allows; in practice the timer runs in the app with an employee session.
steps.code:
const { data: entries } = await taimbox
  .from('time_entries')
  .select('id, allocation_id, employee_id, date, hours, notes')
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .gte('date', '2026-03-01')
  .lte('date', '2026-03-31')
  .order('date')

steps.title: Insert hours manually
steps.description: POST works when RLS allows it (employee linked to the user or agency policies).
steps.note: For bulk integrations, exporting hours_actual from allocations or timer_sessions webhooks via Edge Functions is often more reliable.
steps.code:
const { data, error } = await taimbox
  .from('time_entries')
  .insert({
    allocation_id: allocationId,
    employee_id: employeeId,
    date: '2026-03-10',
    hours: 2.5,
    notes: 'Layout review',
    agency_id: '<AGENCY_ID>'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Timer in the app
steps.description: active_timers holds the running timer; timer_sessions records each stop with exact start/end.
steps.note: Not available with integration API tokens. Use log_timer_hours from an app session or query allocations.hours_actual for aggregated reports.

steps.title: Export hours for billing
steps.description: Join time_entries (or hours_actual) with projects for ERP or invoicing.
steps.code:
const { data: entries } = await taimbox
  .from('time_entries')
  .select('date, hours, allocation_id, allocations(project_id, projects(name))')
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .gte('date', '2026-03-01')

const total = entries?.reduce((s, e) => s + Number(e.hours), 0) ?? 0
console.log(`March total: ${total} h`)

configuration.title: Agency configuration
configuration.subtitle: Manage department_config (weekly close, default view) and client_settings (limits, grouping).
configuration.nextSuffix: employees report when closing the week.

steps.title: Read department settings
steps.description: Each department can define weekly close day/hour and a forced default view.
steps.note: closing_day: 0=Sunday … 6=Saturday. closing_hour: 0–23.
steps.code:
const { data: depts } = await taimbox
  .from('department_config')
  .select('id, department_name, default_view, closing_day, closing_hour, is_view_strict')
  .eq('agency_id', '<AGENCY_ID>')

steps.title: Update weekly close
steps.description: Sync close policy from HR or ops tooling.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('department_config')
  .update({ closing_day: 4, closing_hour: 14 })
  .eq('id', departmentConfigId)
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Advanced client settings
steps.description: client_settings uses client_id as PK: budget cap, commercial group, visibility.
steps.code:
const { data: settings } = await taimbox
  .from('client_settings')
  .select('client_id, budget_limit, group_name, is_hidden, is_sales_account')
  .eq('client_id', clientId)

steps.title: Upsert client_settings
steps.description: Create or update settings without duplicate rows.
steps.note: Handy when importing CRM clients with custom billing rules.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('client_settings')
  .upsert({
    client_id: clientId,
    budget_limit: 5000,
    group_name: 'Premium',
    is_sales_account: true
  })
  .select()
  .single()

feedback.title: Weekly feedback
feedback.subtitle: Read and write weekly_feedback: blockers, bad estimates, and weekly close actions (weekly_action).

steps.title: List feedback for a week
steps.description: Filter by employee_id and week_start_date (Monday).
steps.note: reason: technical_issue, client_blocker, bad_estimation, personal_absence, other.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('weekly_feedback')
  .select('id, project_id, allocation_id, reason, weekly_action, comments')
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .eq('week_start_date', '2026-03-02')
  .order('created_at')

steps.title: Report a blocker
steps.description: Log an issue tied to a project or specific allocation.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('weekly_feedback')
  .insert({
    employee_id: employeeId,
    week_start_date: '2026-03-02',
    project_id: projectId,
    allocation_id: allocationId,
    reason: 'client_blocker',
    comments: 'Waiting for client approval'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Weekly close action
steps.description: weekly_action records what the employee chose at close: keep, postpone, distribute, transfer, justify, cancel, move.
steps.note: Executing the action (e.g. transfer) may require app flows; the feedback row is the audit trail.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('weekly_feedback')
  .update({ weekly_action: 'postpone', comments: 'Move to next week' })
  .eq('id', feedbackId)
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Export feedback for retros
steps.description: Aggregate by reason or project for ops dashboards.

goals.title: Goals & routines
goals.subtitle: Sync professional_goals (personal OKRs) and user_routines (recurring employee tasks).

steps.title: Read professional goals
steps.description: Each employee can have goals with key_results, dates, and 0–100 progress.
steps.code:
const { data: goals } = await taimbox
  .from('professional_goals')
  .select('id, title, key_results, progress, start_date, due_date')
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .order('due_date')

steps.title: Create a goal
steps.description: Import targets from people ops or an LMS.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('professional_goals')
  .insert({
    employee_id: employeeId,
    title: 'Google Ads certification',
    key_results: [{ title: 'Pass exam', done: false }],
    start_date: '2026-01-01',
    due_date: '2026-06-30',
    progress: 0
  })
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Update progress
steps.description: Sync advances from external reviews.
steps.code:
const { data } = await taimbox
  .from('professional_goals')
  .update({ progress: 50 })
  .eq('id', goalId)
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Recurring routines (user_routines)
steps.description: Daily standups, weekly reviews, etc. Optionally linked to project_id.
steps.code:
const { data: routines } = await taimbox
  .from('user_routines')
  .select('id, title, estimated_minutes, project_id, is_active')
  .eq('employee_id', employeeId)
  .eq('is_active', true)

const { data: routine } = await taimbox
  .from('user_routines')
  .insert({
    employee_id: employeeId,
    title: 'Daily standup',
    estimated_minutes: 15
  })
  .select()
  .single()

locks.title: Editing locks
locks.subtitle: Query and manage project_editing_locks to avoid conflicts when editing the same project/month.
locks.doneText: You’ve completed every tutorial aligned with the reference. Open the
locks.doneSuffix: for column details, sample responses, and RLS limits.

steps.title: Check active locks
steps.description: A valid lock has expires_at in the future (expires after 5 minutes).
steps.code:
const { data: locks } = await taimbox
  .from('project_editing_locks')
  .select('id, project_id, employee_id, month, locked_at, expires_at')
  .eq('project_id', projectId)
  .eq('month', '2026-03')
  .gt('expires_at', new Date().toISOString())

steps.title: Acquire a lock
steps.description: Before editing deadlines or collaborative planning, the app creates a lock per project/month.
steps.note: Server-to-server integrations usually only need reads; planning edits remain mainly in the app.
steps.code:
const { data: lock } = await taimbox
  .from('project_editing_locks')
  .insert({
    project_id: projectId,
    employee_id: employeeId,
    month: '2026-03'
  })
  .select()
  .single()

steps.title: Release or wait for expiry
steps.description: Delete the lock when done or let it expire on its own.
steps.note: If you hit a conflict, another employee holds the active lock for that project_id + month.
steps.code:
await taimbox
  .from('project_editing_locks')
  .delete()
  .eq('id', lockId)
